channel.title: nosql on Abhishek's blog
channel.description: Recent content in nosql on Abhishek's blog

item.title: Use AWS App Runner, DynamoDB and CDK to deploy and run a Cloud native Go app
item.description: Earlier, I wrote about a Serverless URL shortener application on AWS using DynamoDB, AWS Lambda and API Gateway. In this blog post, we will deploy that as a REST API on AWS App Runner and continue to use DynamoDB as the database.

item.title: Build a Serverless URL shortener with Go
item.description: Using AWS Lambda, DynamoDB and API Gateway This blog post covers how to build a Serverless URL shortener application using Go.

item.title: Learn how to use DynamoDB Streams with AWS Lambda and Go
item.description: Replicate DynamoDB data from one table to another This blog post will help you get quickly started with DynamoDB Streams and AWS Lambda using Go.

item.title: MySQL to DynamoDB: Build a streaming data pipeline on AWS using Kafka
item.description: Use change data capture with MSK Connect to sync data between Aurora MySQL and DynamoDB This is the second part of the blog series which provides a step-by-step walkthrough of data pipelines with Kafka and Kafka Connect.

item.title: Build a data pipeline on AWS with Kafka, Kafka connect and DynamoDB
item.description: Integrate DynamoDB with MSK and MSK Connect There are many ways to stitch data pipelines - open source components, managed services, ETL tools, etc.

item.title: Build a Twitter Leaderboard app with Redis and AWS Lambda (part 2)
item.description: This is the second blog post of this two-part series that uses a practical application to demonstrate how to integrate Redis with AWS Lambda.

item.title: Build Cloud-Native apps with AWS App Runner, Redis and AWS CDK
item.description: Run your Go app on AWS App Runner service, integrate it with MemoryDB for Redis and use AWS CDK to package and deploy the app along with infrastructure

item.title: Build a Twitter Leaderboard app with Redis and AWS Lambda (part 1)
item.description: Hello and welcome 👋🏼 to this two-part blog series that uses a practical application to demonstrate how to integrate Redis with AWS Lambda.

item.title: Getting started with Redis on AWS - the easy way!
item.description: This quick-start uses AWS Cloud9 IDE to help you get up and running with MemoryDB for Redis, quickly When I was initially exploring some of the AWS services (such as MemoryDB, MSK, Elasticache for Redis etc.

item.title: Now Generally Available – Partial Document Update in Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Originally posted on https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/partial-document-update-ga/ We’re excited to announce the general availability of partial document update for the Azure Cosmos DB Core (SQL) API, which was announced at Microsoft Ignite!

item.title: Extracting Value from IoT Using Azure Cosmos DB, Azure Synapse Analytics, and Confluent Cloud
item.description: It’s possible to build seamless device-to-cloud experience with the integrations between Azure and Confluent Cloud. The example provided in this blog post showcases how the following capabilities of Azure and Confluent Cloud are leveraged:

item.title: Getting started with Kafka Connector for Azure Cosmos DB using Docker
item.description: Having a local development environment is quite handy when trying out a new service or technology. Docker has emerged as the de-facto choice in such cases.

item.title: Processing Time-Series Data with Redis and Apache Kafka
item.description: RedisTimeSeries is a Redis Module that brings native Time Series data structure to Redis. Time Series solutions which were earlier built on top of Sorted Sets (or Redis Streams) can benefit from RedisTimeSeries features such as high volume inserts, low latency reads, flexible query language, down-sampling and much more!

item.title: Autoscaling Redis applications on Kubernetes 🚀🚀
item.description: This blog post demonstrates how to auto-scale your Redis based applications on Kubernetes. Redis is a widely used (and loved!) database which supports a rich set of data structures (String, Hash, Streams, Geospatial), as well as other features such as pub/sub messaging, clustering (HA) etc.

item.title: Azure Cosmos DB: Use Cases and Trade-Offs
item.description: Azure Cosmos DB is a fully managed, elastically scalable and globally distributed database with a multi-model approach, and provides you with the ability to use document, key-value, wide-column, or graph-based data.

item.title: An easy to use monitoring solution for Redis
item.description: Recently, I discovered a nice way of plugging in monitoring for Redis using Grafana, thanks to this great Data Source plugin that works with any Redis database, including Azure Cache for Redis!

item.title: Getting started with Rust and Redis
item.description: Are you learning Rust and looking for ways to get some hands-on practice with concrete examples? A good approach might be to try and integrate Rust with external systems.

item.title: Build fault tolerant applications with Cassandra API for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Azure Cosmos DB is a resource governed system that allows you to execute a certain number of operations per second based on the provisioned throughput you have configured.

item.title: How to use Azure Go SDK to manage Azure Data Explorer clusters
item.description: Getting started with Azure Data Explorer using the Go SDK covered how to use the Azure Data Explorer Go SDK to ingest and query data from azure data explorer to ingest and query data.

item.title: Tutorial: Getting started with Azure Data Explorer using the Go SDK
item.description: With the help of an example, this blog post will walk you through how to use the Azure Data explorer Go SDK to ingest data from a Azure Blob storage container and query it programmatically using the SDK.

item.title: Data pipeline using MongoDB and Kafka Connect on Kubernetes
item.description: In Kafka Connect on Kubernetes, the easy way!, I had demonstrated Kafka Connect on Kubernetes using Strimzi along with the File source and sink connector.
